source_metadata.title_raw: Robert Garcia - A-19-055 - May 20, 2019 - Jurupa Valley

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Robert Garcia 
Board of Education President 
Jurupa Unified School District 
8524 Wild Pony Drive 
Jurupa Valley, CA 92509 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-19-055 
Dear Mr. Garcia: 
May 20, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding Government Code Section 1090 
and the conflict of interest provisions of and the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 Please note that 
we are only providing advice under Section 1090 et seq. and the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Act and not under any other conflict of interest laws. Also note that we are not a finder of fact 
when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes 
your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions 
should change, you should contact us for additional advice. 
In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to it to the Office of the Attorney General and the Riverside County District 
Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097 .1 ( c )(3 ). ) We are also required to specify 
whether we have received a written response from either entity: we have not. (Section 
1097.l(c)(4).) Finally, we are required to advise you that, for purposes of Section 1090, the 
following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against any individual other than the 
requestor." (Section 1097.l(c)(5).) 
QUESTION 
Does either the Act or Section 1090 prohibit you, Jurupa Unified School District ("JUSD") 
Board of Education President Robert Garcia, from making or participating in the making of a 
potential contract between the JUSD and Hoc~iki America Corporation ("Hochiki") for fire alarm 
and voice communications systems equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School 
given that you are a Hochiki employee? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


CONCLUSION 
File No. A-19-055 
Page No. 2 
Yes. Section 1090 prohibits you from making or participating in the making of, and the 
JUSD Board of Education from entering into, a contract with Hochiki for fire alarm and voice 
communications systems equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School because you 
are a Hochiki employee with a financial interest in any such prospective contract. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
You are the President of the JUSD Board of Education. The JUSD is located in the 
northwest portion of the County of Riverside. You are a Quality Assurance Manager for Hochiki, a 
business entity that manufactures and sells fire-protection equipment and provides related services. 
You were elected to the JUSD Board of Education in November 2014, and you began working at 
Hochiki on July 1, 2016. Hochiki has submitted a proposal to provide fire alarm and voice 
communications system equipment and related services, excluding installation, at Peralta 
Elementary School, a JUSD school located in the City of Riverside, for an estimated contract price 
of $31,258.00. The potential contract was not competitively bid, and there are currently no other 
business entities pursuing it. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 1090 prohibits a public officer or employee from making or participating in the 
making of a contract in which he or she is financially interested. Section 1090 is concerned with 
financial interests, other than remote interests or noninterests, that prevent a public officer or 
employee from exercising absolute loyalty and undivided allegiance in furthering the best interests 
of his or her agency. (Stigall v. Taft (1962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 569.) A contract that violates Section 
1090 is void. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646.) 
The California Supreme Court has stated that the purpose of Section 1090 is to make certain 
that "every public officer be guided solely by the public interest, rather than by personal interest, 
when dealing with contracts in an official capacity." (Id. at p. 650.) Eliminating temptation for 
public officers and employees, avoiding the perception of impropriety, and obtaining their 
undivided loyalty are important public policy goals in California. (Id. at p. 648.) Consequently, 
Section 1090 applies without regard to whether actual fraud or dishonesty is involved in the 
contract process, the contract is fair to the public agency, or the public agency loses money from the 
contract. (Id. at pp. 648-649.) 
Under Section 1090, a member of a public entity's governing body is conclusively 
presumed to participate in the making of a contract under the body's authority, irrespective of 
whether he or she actually participates in the making·of the contract at issue. (Id. at pp. 649-650.) 
Therefore, Section 1090 prohibits the entire body from entering into a contract in which a member 
of the body is financially interested, even if that member abstains from participating in the making 
of the contract or fully discloses his or her financial interest in the contract. (Ibid.) In other words, 
where Section 1090 applies, it prohibits the entire governing body from entering into that contract. 


File No. A-19-055 
Page No. 3 
You are subject to Section 1090 because you are a member of the JUSD Board of Education 
and Education Code Section 35233 provides, among other things, that Section 1090 applies to 
members of governing boards of school districts. Decisions relating to the potential contract 
between the JUSD and.Hochiki for equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School 
involve that contract. As explained above, under Section 1090, you would be conclusively 
presumed to participate in the making of that contract because you are a member of the JUSD 
Board of Education, the governing body authorized to enter into that contract. You would also have 
a financial interest in that contract because a public officer has a financial interest in a contract 
between his or her public entity and his or her private employer. (89 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 278, 280 
(2006); 88 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 106, 108-110 (2005); 58 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 670, 677-678 (1975).) 
Thus, Section 1090 prohibits you from making or participating in the making of, and the 
JUSD Board of Education from entering into, the potential contract between the JUSD and Hochiki 
for equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School, unless a remote-interest exception, 
noninterest exception, or the rule of necessity applies. 
Remote Interest and Noninterest Exceptions 
The Legislature has created various statutory• exceptions to Section 1090' s prohibition where 
the financial interest involved is deemed to be a "remote interest," as defined in Section 1091, or a 
"noninterest," as defined in Section 1091.5. There is no potentially applicable noninterest that 
merits discussion with respect to the facts presented. There are, however, two potentially applicable 
remote interests, and we now consider whether they apply. 
Section 1091 (b )(2) provides a remote interest applicable to an employee or agent of a 
contracting party if all the following conditions are met: 
• 
The contracting party at issue has 10 or more other employees; 
• 
The public officer at issue was an employee or agent of the contracting party for at least three 
years prior to the officer initially accepting his or her office; 
• 
The public officer at issue owns less than three percent of the contracting party's shares of 
stock; and 
• 
The public officer at issue is not an officer or director of the contracting party and did not 
directly participate in formulating the bid of the contracting party. 
You were elected to the JUSD Board of Education in November 2014, and you began 
working at Hochiki on July 1, 2016. Therefore, you were not an employee or agent ofHochiki for 
three years prior to initially becoming a member of the JUSD Board of Education based on the facts 
presented. Thus, the remote interest of Section 1091(b)(2) does not apply. 
Section 1091 (b )(3) sets forth a remote interest applicable to an employee or agent of a 
contracting party if all the following conditions are met: 


File No. A-19-055 
Page No. 4 
• 
The public officer at issue's local public agency is located in a county with a population ofless 
than 4,000,000; 
• 
The public contract at issue is competitively bid and is not for personal services; 
• 
The public officer is not in a primary management capacity with, is not an officer or director of, 
and holds no ownership interest in, the contracting party; 
• 
The contracting party has 10 or more other employees; 
• 
The public officer did not directly participate in formulating the contracting party's bid; and 
• 
The contracting party is the lowest responsible bidder. 
Based on the facts presented, the potential contract between the JUSD and Hochiki for 
equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School was not competitively bid, and there 
are currently no other business entities pursuing that contract. Thus, the remote interest of Section 
1091(b)(2) does not apply. 
Rule of Necessity 
In limited circumstances, the "rule of necessity" applies to allow the making of a contract 
that Section 1090 would otherwise prohibit. (88 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 106, 110 (2005).) In 
procurement situations, the rule of necessity has permitted a government agency to acquire an 
essential supply or service despite a conflict of interest when the business in which the public 
officer has an interest is the sole source of supply of the essential supply or service available. (65 
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 305, 310 (1982).) With respect to its application in procurement situations, the 
rule of necessity has only been applied "in extreme cases of emergency, or where no alternative 
source of supply of goods or services existed." (59 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 604,619, fn. 18 (1976).) 
You have not presented any facts indicating an emergency or that there is no alternative 
source of the equipment and related services at issue. Therefore, the rule of necessity is not 
applicable based on the facts presented.2 
Thus, Section 1090 prohibits you from making or participating in making, and the JUSD 
Board of Education from entering into, the potential contract between the JUSD and Hochiki for 
equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School. 3 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
2 You should seek additional advice ifthere are facts supporting the rule ofnecessity's application to the 
potential contract between the JUSD and Hochiki for equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School. 
3 We do not analyze the Act's conflict of interest provisions with respect to governmental decisions relating to 
the potential contract between the JUSD and Hochiki for equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School 
because we have already determined that Section 1090 prohibits you from making or participating in making that 
contract. 


MFC:aja 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
File No. A-19-055 
Page No. 5 
By: 
Matthew F. Christy 
Counsel, Legal Division

Yes. Section 1090 prohibits you from making or participating in the making of, and the 
JUSD Board of Education from entering into, a contract with Hochiki for fire alarm and voice 
communications systems equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School because you 
are a Hochiki employee with a financial interest in any such prospective contract.

Section 1091 (b )(3) sets forth a remote interest applicable to an employee or agent of a

•

than 4,000,000; 
• 
The public contract at issue is competitively bid and is not for personal services; 
•

and holds no ownership interest in, the contracting party;

potential contract between the JUSD and Hoc~iki America Corporation ("Hochiki") for fire alarm 
and voice communications systems equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School 
given that you are a Hochiki employee?

CONCLUSION: Yes. Section 1090 prohibits you from making or participating in the making of, and the 
JUSD Board of Education from entering into, a contract with Hochiki for fire alarm and voice 
communications systems equipment and related services at Peralta Elementary School because you 
are a Hochiki employee with a financial interest in any such prospective contract.
